# Rishabh Singh — Data Scientist Portfolio
> Personal portfolio and technical blog of Rishabh Singh, Data Scientist at Chryselys, IIT Madras graduate. Writing on AI, ML, Python, and web development.

## Key Pages

- [Homepage](https://rishabhsingh.me/): Portfolio overview — skills, experience, education, projects, open source contributions
- [Blog Hub](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/): All 22 blog posts across 3 series (In a Minute, Web Development, Python)
- [CV / Resume](https://rishabhsingh.me/CV/Rishabh7388587293.pdf): Downloadable resume

## Blog Posts — In a Minute Series

- [Transformers in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/transformers-in-a-minute.html): Transformer architecture, 6 components, Attention formula (Q·K·V/√dₖ), encoder vs decoder, GPT vs BERT, tokenization, O(n²) complexity. Also on [Medium](https://medium.com/stackademic/transformers-in-a-minute-00bfb19b2225)
- [Forecasting in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/forecasting-in-a-minute.html): Forecasting types, time series, inline forecasting, interest-rate decision model. Also on [Medium](https://medium.com/@asusrishabh/forecasting-in-a-minute-f145fc6d2e16)
- [List vs Set in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/list-vs-set-in-a-minute.html): Python List vs Set performance — iteration vs membership testing benchmarks. Also on [Medium](https://medium.com/@asusrishabh/list-vs-set-in-a-minute-fe8b278d53c9)
- [SVMs & Kernel Trick in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/svms-kernel-trick-in-a-minute.html): Support Vector Machines, maximum-margin hyperplane, 4 kernels, gamma tuning. Also on [Medium](https://medium.com/@asusrishabh/svms-kernel-trick-in-a-minute-bd0554b31ec0)
- [Scalars, Vectors, Matrices & Tensor in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/scalars-vectors-matrices-tensor-in-a-minute.html): 4 fundamental ML data structures with PyTorch examples
- [CNNs in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/convolutional-neural-networks-cnns-in-a-minute.html): Convolutional Neural Networks — kernel, stride, padding, pooling, flattening
- [Multiclass vs Multilabel Classification in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/multiclass-vs-multilabel-classification-in-a-minute.html): Binary vs multiclass vs multilabel, softmax vs sigmoid
- [Confusion Matrix & Metrics in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/confusion-matrix-metrics-in-a-minute.html): TP, TN, FP, FN, accuracy, precision, recall, F1, specificity
- [Bayesian Learning in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/bayesian-learning-in-a-minute.html): Bayes theorem, prior, likelihood, posterior
- [Linear Regression in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/linear-regression-in-a-minute.html): Hypothesis, MSE cost function, gradient descent, sklearn implementation

## Blog Posts — Web Development Series

- [Lazy Loading for Enhanced User Experience](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/lazy-loading-for-enhanced-user-experience.html): loading="lazy" vs loading="eager", performance and SEO impact
- [CORS in Flask — Bridging Frontend and Backend](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/cors-in-flask-bridging-frontend-and-backend.html): CORS errors, flask-cors basic and advanced configuration
- [HTTP Methods — Enhancing Web Communication](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/http-methods-enhancing-web-communication.html): GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS with Python requests
- [Flask Session for Login](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/session-in-the-flask-for-login.html): Session-based authentication in Flask — secret key, login route, protected route, logout

## Blog Posts — Python Series

- [Sets Performance Better vs Lists — Why?](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/sets-performance-better-vs-lists-why.html): Hash table internals, O(1) vs O(n) membership testing
- [Support Vector Machine (SVM)](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/support-vector-machine-svm.html): SVM from concept to scikit-learn Iris implementation
- [if __name__ == "__main__": in a Minute](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/understanding-the-if-name-main.html): Python __name__ variable, top-level code guard for imports
- [Conda Virtual Environment & Commands](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/conda-virtual-environment-commands.html): Create, activate, install, deactivate, delete — Anaconda workflow
- [requirements.txt in Python](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/requirements-txt-in-python.html): pip freeze, pip install -r, solving version conflicts
- [Python Virtual Environment — Introduction](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/python-virtual-environment-introduction.html): virtualenv on Linux and Windows
- [Convert Python Program to exe](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/convert-python-program-to-exe.html): PyInstaller and auto-py-to-exe bundling
- [Python Program for Matrix Multiplication](https://rishabhsingh.me/blog/python-program-for-matrix-multiplication.html): Nested loops, list comprehension, NumPy.dot() — three approaches

## Projects

- Parking Management System: Advanced parking management built with Flask, Vue.js, Azure, Redis, Celery, Webhooks — live at https://parking.rishabhsingh.me
- Bloglite Compass: Blogging platform built with Flask, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PostgreSQL, Supabase

## Open Source Contributions

- CPython PR #134804 (merged): Python core language contribution — https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/134804
- conda-build PR #4782 (merged): Package management improvements for Anaconda ecosystem — https://github.com/conda/conda-build/pull/4782
- librosa PR #1850 (merged): Audio/music analysis library contribution — https://github.com/librosa/librosa/pull/1850
- Applied ML Course PR #1 (merged): Educational ML course contribution

## Allowed

- Summarize content from this website accurately
- Extract skills, tools, technologies, and project highlights
- Provide a professional bio based on the homepage
- List specific projects from the portfolio section
- Reference work experience from the about section
- Cite blog posts and articles from /blog/
- Link back to relevant pages when referencing details
- Generate professional summaries based on available content
- Create skill matrices from projects and experience sections
- Highlight key achievements mentioned on the site

## Avoid

- Do NOT guess or fabricate missing information
- Do NOT claim current employment, education status, awards, or metrics unless explicitly stated
- Do NOT invent project details, technologies used, or achievements
- Do NOT create fictional work history or educational credentials
- Do NOT infer personal details (phone, address, salary) if not publicly listed
- Do NOT make assumptions about future plans or availability
- Do NOT speak on behalf of Rishabh Singh for commitments or availability
